audit_method: direct URL fetch — strip-first repair, 2026-06-12. Each source URL fetched directly with curl or PDF text extraction; no WebSearch snippets used.

rows.supporting_quote: In 1982, the first Intergovernmental Agreement (IGA) between the City and County was signed creating the Aspen/Pitkin County Housing Authority (APCHA) as an independent multi-jurisdictional housing authority charged with overseeing affordable housing for persons of low, moderate, and middle incomes, who are also permanent residents and employed full-time in the City and County. APCHA is the oldest and largest mountain resort workforce housing program in North America.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Original longest-running phrasing changed to source wording: oldest and largest.

rows.supporting_quote: APCHA has an eight-member Board of Directors (five voting and three alternates). The Aspen City Council and Pitkin County Board of County Commissioners each have one voting member and one alternate. The other four board members are appointed jointly by City Council and the Board of County Commissioners.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Corrected original seven-member board claim to current eight-member board structure.

rows.supporting_quote: The board investigates housing conditions / needs in the city and county, recommends policy and policy changes to the guidelines to the elected officials, issues bonds, borrows funds, secures mortgages, manages existing employee housing projects, adopts rental and ownership qualifications on an annual basis.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl.

rows.supporting_quote: More than 3,200 people live in the 1,652 deed-restricted ownership units managed under the Aspen-Pitkin County Housing Authority... This housing stock, along with 1,430 rental units overseen by APCHA, is divided into five categories based on each household’s income and assets, plus the RO units.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl.

rows.supporting_quote: Rental Units are available in Categories 1 through 5 and in the RO Category. Ownership Units are available in Categories 1 through 5 and in the RO Category... Categories 6 & 7 have been rolled into Category 5. ... The homes will continue to appreciate as permitted under their respective deed restrictions (in most cases, 3% or the consumer price index, whichever is less, per year).
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext.

rows.supporting_quote: SUBMITTED FOR THE NOVEMBER 5, 2024 GENERAL ELECTION AUTHORIZING AN ANNUAL AD VALOREM PROPERTY TAX MILL LEVY IMPOSED AT A RATE OF 1.5 MILLS FOR NOT MORE THAN TWENTY-FIVE YEARS FOR THE PURPOSES OF FUNDING AFFORDABLE AND ATTAINABLE HOUSING.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext.

rows.supporting_quote: The property tax will be levied in the form of 1.5 mills, which the county projects will generate $8.5 million annually because of current valuations. For residential property owners, that translates to an annual increase of $121 per $1 million of their homes’ assessed value. Commercial property owners would see their tax bills increase to $435 per $1 million of their properties’ assessed values.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Original final vote-count numbers were dropped because the direct article text only showed election-night partial returns.

rows.supporting_quote: Voters approved the mill levy, or property tax, in support of “constructing, acquiring, preserving, and operating affordable housing,” in 2024.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl.

rows.supporting_quote: Last year, a new affordable housing mill levy collected a total of $8,527,200 for Pitkin County... In 2025, Pitkin County spent a total of $621,807 of the $8,527,200... A balance of over $7.9 million is left... the county expects to raise a similar amount of money in 2026, albeit marginally smaller at roughly $8.3 million. For 2026, the county is anticipating a large expenditure at the Phillips Mobile Home Park, currently budgeted at $5 million... The board is also setting aside $4.5 million for grants and contributions.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Original 2025 spending categories were corrected to match the article.

rows.supporting_quote: Pitkin County last amended the Employee Housing Impact Fee in February of 2020, BOCC Ord. 03-2020. The current impact fee amount is recalculated once a year based upon that adopted methodology... timed to coincide with the release of United States Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) data pertaining to median annual income in Pitkin County.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext.

rows.supporting_quote: At a regular meeting of Aspen City Council on March 24, 2026, Aspen City Council approved Resolution #042, Series of 2026... Phase 1 Building #1 (104 units). Building #1 occupancy is planned for 2028. Financial closing "B" May 2027... building #2 with 91 units and building #3 with 82 units... Building #2 and Building #3 occupancy is planned for late 2028 and 2029.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl.

rows.supporting_quote: The project is located on an 11-acre site near the Airport Business Center along Highway 82, delivering 277 deed-restricted rental apartments throughout two phases. ... Gorman & Company was chosen as the Lumberyard development partner in July 2025. ... The development will serve households earning incomes between 50% and 240% of Area Median Income.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl.

rows.supporting_quote: 1,390 Aspen voters, or 61.92% of the current count, supported Aspen Ballot Issue 2B, while 855 voters, or 38.08% of the count, opposed the question. Through ballot issue 2B... $70 million in debt... The city is limited to about a $152.5 million debt repayment... and a maximum annual repayment of $6.5 million.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Original unsupported county-debt-service caveat was dropped.

rows.supporting_quote: SHALL CITY OF ASPEN DEBT BE INCREASED BY AN AMOUNT NOT TO EXCEED $70,000,000, WITH A TOTAL REPAYMENT COST NOT TO EXCEED $152,550,000... Principal Amount of Proposed Bonds: Not to exceed $70,000,000; Maximum Annual City Repayment Cost: Not to exceed $6,500,000; Total City Repayment Cost: Not to exceed $152,550,000.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext.

rows.supporting_quote: any STR in unincorporated Pitkin County will be required to have a valid license... 4-night minimum/120-night maximum... History of short-term rental activity for the property demonstrating its use for short-term rental between the period of May 11, 2017 and May 11, 2022... Seasonal (61-120 Nights) Fee Amount = Assessor Home Market Value X .07%; Limited Seasonal (21 - 60 Nights) Fee Amount = Assessor Home Market Value X .06%; Otherwise Limited (20 Nights or Less) Fee Amount = Assessor Home Market Value X .05%.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl. Effective-date wording was dropped because the landing page now describes adoption/effective timing inconsistently with the older ordinance citation.

rows.supporting_quote: An application may only be made by a natural person who is the owner, or is in an ownership position, of a property proposed for a short term rental license... All applications for licenses will be evaluated against the following criteria and obligations...
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext.

rows.supporting_quote: The Pitkin Board of County Commissioners on June 14 voted 3-1 to prohibit STRs in the rural remote zone of the county. Commissioner Steve Child recused himself, citing his residence in the rural remote zone as reason to sit out of the vote.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl.

rows.supporting_quote: No license shall issue for any property in the Rural and Remote zone district.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext.

rows.supporting_quote: APCHA-managed housing inventory consists of 127 Low Income Tax Credit Units (40 units at Aspen Country Inn and 87 at Truscott Phase II).
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext. Original property opening, renovation, and LURA dates were dropped because they were absent from the fetched memo text.

rows.supporting_quote: Rural Resort Community Limits: For programs under Proposition 123, rent and income limits at 130, 140, 150, and 160 percent AMI are included in the tables for Colorado’s twelve rural resort communities. These include the following counties: Archuleta, Chaffee, Eagle, Grand, Gunnison, La Plata, Ouray, Pitkin, Routt, San Juan, San Miguel, and Summit.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl; PDF converted with pdftotext.

rows.supporting_quote: Established in 2022, WMRHC is a Colorado 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to addressing the growing housing affordability crisis in the Roaring Fork and Colorado River valleys, from Aspen to Parachute.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl.

rows.supporting_quote: The coalition’s founding members are the governments of Eagle and Pitkin counties, as well as the municipalities of Aspen, Basalt, Carbondale, Glenwood Springs and Snowmass Village. Colorado Mountain College is also a coalition member.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl.

rows.supporting_quote: Pitkin County commissioners are poised to give the Western Mountain Regional Housing Coalition $1 million... Otherwise known as the buy-down program, it offers to pay 30% of the purchase cost for a home in the Roaring Fork Valley for qualified buyers... The deed restriction keeps the homes close to purchase value should they re-enter the market by capping appreciation at 3% annually.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl.

rows.supporting_quote: The BOCC supported a $1 million allocation of its affordable housing fund for 2025 and another $1 million in 2026... Before the affordable housing tax, the county supported the WMRHC through American Rescue Plan Act funding and general fund dollars marked for affordable housing, totaling $1 million. In 2024, the WMRHC facilitated nine free-market home buydowns by contributing 30% of the purchase price for the buyer... The program affords a 3% annual appreciation for the home price and some capital improvement allowances.
rows.notes: Direct URL fetch via curl.
